FT_IC1.evidence: Amsterdam Born Children and their Development (ABCD) study in the Netherlands, not U.S. ABCD

FT_IC2.evidence: TV and video games measured but no smartphone or social media variables; generic screen time only

FT_EC7_reason: Paper fails FT-IC1 (wrong ABCD cohort); exclusion via population criterion
notes: Excluded at FT-IC1: uses Amsterdam ABCD (Dutch cohort), not U.S. ABCD Study

FT_IC1.evidence: Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) study, U.S., N=4277 with usable imaging and SMA data from first cross-sectional release

FT_IC2.evidence: Screen media activity (SMA) measured: watching TV/videos, playing video games, using social media; composite score reported plus component breakdown

FT_IC3.evidence: Group Factor Analysis (GFA) with generalized linear mixed-effect models testing associations between SMA latent variables and psychopathology (internalizing/externalizing) and cognition (fluid/crystalized intelligence); multiple inferential tests reported

FT_IC4.evidence: Cohen's d effect sizes reported (ES 0.04-0.1); 37% variance explained in SMA-brain relationship; multiple effect size statistics in Table 1 and results

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical cross-sectional analysis with multivariate structural analysis testing association between SMA and brain structure outcomes

FT_IC6.evidence: Published journal article (NeuroImage 2019); no evidence of duplicate

FT_EC7_reason: SMA not outcome variable in primary analyses; SMA is predictor of brain structure and psychopathology outcomes (SMA → outcomes direction)
notes: Latent construct analysis: SMA-specific pathways extractable from four GFA factors (E3 applies); mixed-modality SMA composite with separate TV/video games/social media components

FT_IC1.evidence: Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) Study, U.S.; paper discusses current and planned ABCD technologies

FT_IC2.evidence: Mobile phone use, smartphone use, wearable technologies, social media data capture discussed as measurement approaches in ABCD

FT_IC3.evidence: Methods/perspective paper; no inferential statistical tests linking smartphone/SM variables to non-SM outcomes reported; no original empirical associations tested

FT_IC4.evidence: No numeric statistics (β/OR/p-values) reported for smartphone/SM associations; paper is prospective/planning only

FT_IC5.evidence: Not original empirical analysis; perspective/methods paper outlining future potential technologies and data collection approaches, no completed analyses

FT_IC6.evidence: Published journal article (Developmental Cognitive Neuroscience 2018); no evidence of duplicate

FT_EC7_reason: Paper fails FT-IC3 and FT-IC5 (no empirical analyses with inferential tests); exclusion criteria met before FT-EC7 assessment
notes: Excluded: methods/perspective paper with no original empirical analysis (E10 applies). Paper describes technologies currently used and those being piloted in ABCD but contains no statistical tests of smartphone/SM associations with outcomes.
